Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Tatjana Smith will be back in the pool in Paris TODAY: Here’s when

South Africans don’t have to wait long for Tatjana Smith to return to the pool at the Paris La Defense Arena. Only hours in fact!

Tatjana Smithtook victory in the women’s 100m breaststroke at theParis Olympics 2024on Monday evening, giving South Africa its first gold medal of the Games.

Enjoy the Paris Olympic Games 2024 experience at your favourite ATKV Resort!

Smith touched the wall in a time of1:05:28, finishing ahead of China’sQianting Tang(1:05:54), with Ireland’sMona McSharryin third (1:05.59).

Tributes were quick to pour in, including fromchicken fast food chain Nando’s.

South Africans don’t have to wait long for Smith to return to the pool at the Paris La Defense Arena. A couple more hours in fact.

Smith is the defending Olympic champion in thewomen’s 200m breaststrokehaving won gold at the postponed Tokyo Olympics 2020.

In Japan, Smith set a new Olympic and world record in the event, timing2:18.95in the final.

That world record was then smashed by Russian swimmerEvgeniia Chikunova, who set2:17.55in Kazan, Russia in April 2023.

However, Chikunova isNOTat the Paris Olympics 2024.

In fact, only23swimmers are entered into the heats for the women’s 200m breaststroke.

The three heats will take place onWednesday, 31 July, starting at11:03(SA time).

Tatjana Smith and Team South Africa’s second entrant in the event,Kaylene Corbett, have both been drawn in Heat 1.

In something of a waste of time, the 23 starters will be reduced to 16 for the semi-finals starting at21:51(SA time), also on Wednesday, 31 July.

The top 8 will race the final onThursday, 1 Augustat21:04(SA time).

Smith will be the overwhelming favourite to defend her Olympic crown and add a second gold medal to her tally in Paris.

Meanwhile, Smith pocketedR400 000for her win in the women’s 100m breaststroke thanks to the financial incentives promised by theSouth African Sports Confederation and Olympic Committee (Sascoc).

Smith’s coach, Rocco Meiring, will earn R100 000 for his role’s in Smith’s victory.

IndividualsGold medallists R400 000 and the coach R100 000Silver medallists R200 000 and the coach R50 000Bronze medallists R75 000 and the coach R25 000

In addition, as reported byThe South African website, new Minister of Sport, Arts and Culture,Gayton McKenzie, promised that he has a ‘big surprise’ for all Team SA medal winners, suggesting there could be more prize money coming their way.

title: When will Tatjana Smith next be in action at Paris Olympics 2024?
author: Garrin Lambley
description: South Africans don't have to wait long for Tatjana Smith to return to the pool at the Paris La Defense Arena. Only hours in fact!
